title: Percentage Calculator
description: Calculate percentages, find what percent X is of Y, compute increase/decrease, and reverse-calculate original values.

results.empty: Fill in the fields to calculate

howToUse.label: Choose a calculation type
howToUse.description: Select one of the four modes: basic percentage, "what percent", percentage change, or reverse percentage.

howToUse.label: Fill in the values
howToUse.description: Enter the numbers in the provided fields. The result updates automatically.

howToUse.label: Copy the result
howToUse.description: Click the copy icon next to any result to copy it.

faq.question: How do I calculate X% of Y?
faq.answer: Multiply Y by X and divide by 100. For example, 20% of 150 = 150 × 20 ÷ 100 = 30.

faq.question: How do I find what percentage X is of Y?
faq.answer: Divide X by Y and multiply by 100. For example, 30 is what percent of 150? → 30 ÷ 150 × 100 = 20%.

faq.question: How is percentage change calculated?
faq.answer: Percentage change = ((New Value − Old Value) ÷ Old Value) × 100. A positive result means an increase; a negative result means a decrease.

faq.question: What is reverse percentage?
faq.answer: Reverse percentage finds the original value before a percentage was applied. For example, if a price after a 20% increase is 120, the original price was 120 ÷ 1.20 = 100.

faq.question: How precise are the results?
faq.answer: Results are shown to up to 6 significant figures and rounded for readability. For financial calculations requiring exact precision, verify with a dedicated tool.
